commentaries.author: Clement Of Rome
commentaries.content: Workmen; not workmen to whom the fear of God and righteousness appear to be gain; not workmen who "serve their belly; "not workmen who "with fair speeches and pleasant words mislead the hearts of the innocent; "

commentaries.author: Cyril of Jerusalem
commentaries.content: The heretics do this by coating over their poison pills of godless doctrines with the honey of the name of Christ.

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: Flattery is always insidious, deceitful and bland. And a flatterer is well defined by philosophers as a bland enemy. Truth is harsh, bitter, stern, unpleasant and offensive to those who are reproved.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: It seems that Paul is talking here about the Jewish leaders…. Their words sound wonderful, but they are deceptive. However, they do not fool everyone but only the hearts of the simpleminded.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
